signals.headline: Federal Judge Dismisses Trump's $10B Lawsuit Against WSJ, Allows Refiling by April 27 | NO | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Federal Judge Dismisses Trump's $10B Lawsuit Against WSJ, Allows Refiling by April 27 | NO | HIGH confidence**  
A judge tossed out President Trump's lawsuit over WSJ reporting on his letter with a sketch to Jeffrey Epstein's birthday album (publicly released by Congress), granting his team time to amend and refile[3]. No final dismissal or settlement confirmed, limiting immediate market movement.

signals.headline: Germany March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2
signals.raw:
Germany March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2.7% | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Germany's annual inflation rate rose to 2.7% in March 2026, up from 1.9% in February and confirming preliminary estimates, driven by a 7.2% surge in energy prices amid the Iran war.[1][2][4] This highest level since January 2024 (2.9%) exceeds prior forecasts near 2% and could shift prediction markets on ECB policy or euroz

signals.headline: South Korea March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2.2% | YES | HIGH confidence**  
South Korea's annual inflation rate increased to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0% in prior months, below market forecasts of 2.4% and aligning with the central bank's target despite global energy volatility.[3] This contained rise may influence markets on Bank of Korea rate decisions.[3]

signals.headline: US Naval Blockade of Iran Enacted Following Ceasefire Talks Collapse | NO | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
US Naval Blockade of Iran Enacted Following Ceasefire Talks Collapse | NO | HIGH confidence**

The United States has implemented a **naval blockade on Iran's ports and coastal areas**, which came into effect on April 14, 2026.[2] This followed the collapse of U.S.-Iran ceasefire negotiations in Pakistan on April 13, with President Trump announcing the blockade after talks ended without agreement.[

signals.headline: Germany 2026 GDP Growth Forecast Halved to +0
signals.raw:
Germany 2026 GDP Growth Forecast Halved to +0.6% | YES | MED confidence**  
Leading institutes cut Germany's 2026 GDP growth forecast to +0.6% from prior +1.3%, citing the Iran war's energy shock, with 2027 at +0.9% (down from +1.4%).[4] Expansionary fiscal policy offers some offset, potentially moving markets on recession risks.[4]
